Title: Populus PtrbHLH011 Is a Transcriptional Co-Regulator Involved in the Activation of Cell Wall Biosynthesis by Iron Deprivation

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Populus tremula × Populus alba; Populus trichocarpa
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: essential micronutrient for photosynthesis, respiration and development
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Repression by PtrbHLH011 is proposed to limit root-to-shoot iron transport and leaf iron accumulation under normal conditions; derepression under Fe deprivation or PtrbHLH011 knockout supports Fe accumulation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. YSL3 was identified as a direct target repressed by PtrbHLH011. 2. Its promoter is bound and repressed by PtrbHLH011 in transient assays. 3. Expression decreases in PtrbHLH011 overexpression plants and increases in knockout plants, consistent with a role in Fe translocation/homeostasis.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient ChIP-seq; promoter transactivation assay; qRT-PCR in overexpression and knockout plants
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: leaf and stem expression responsive to PtrbHLH011 perturbation
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential downstream target to improve iron translocation without full PtrbHLH011 knockout
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq GEO: GSE265823; transient ChIP-seq GEO: GSE265825
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as homologous to Arabidopsis YSL genes required for long-distance Fe deficiency signaling.

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Populus tremula × Populus alba; Populus trichocarpa
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: essential micronutrient for photosynthesis, respiration and development
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Direct repression by PtrbHLH011 is proposed to limit Fe deficiency signaling and iron translocation under normal conditions; derepression under Fe deprivation contributes to improved iron homeostasis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PYE was identified as a direct target of PtrbHLH011 by binding and promoter repression assays. 2. PYE expression is reduced in PtrbHLH011 overexpression stems and generally increased in knockout plants. 3. The paper links PYE to Fe deficiency signaling and root-to-shoot iron translocation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient ChIP-seq; promoter transactivation assay; qRT-PCR in overexpression and knockout plants
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: stem-sensitive; major function suggested in root and stem
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential regulatory node downstream of PtrbHLH011 for tuning Fe deficiency signaling
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq GEO: GSE265823; transient ChIP-seq GEO: GSE265825
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although itself a TF, in this paper it is a downstream target rather than the central experimentally interrogated regulator; categorized here as Common_Gene.

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Populus tremula × Populus alba; Populus trichocarpa
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: iron storage/homeostasis and oxidative stress buffering
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PtrbHLH011 repression of FER1 is linked to limiting shoot Fe accumulation; derepression is associated with increased leaf Fe accumulation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FER1 was identified as a direct repression target of PtrbHLH011. 2. PtrbHLH011 binds and represses the FER1 promoter in transient assays. 3. FER1 expression is down in PtrbHLH011 overexpression plants and up in knockout plants, consistent with contribution to Fe accumulation and oxidative stress response.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient ChIP-seq; promoter transactivation assay; qRT-PCR in overexpression and knockout plants
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: leaf and stem expression responsive to PtrbHLH011 perturbation
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential downstream target to enhance leaf iron accumulation and stress buffering
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq GEO: GSE265823; transient ChIP-seq GEO: GSE265825
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Ferritin regulation may also contribute to oxidative stress responses under Fe deprivation.

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Populus tremula × Populus alba; Populus trichocarpa
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PtrbHLH011 repression of PtIRT3 is linked to limiting shoot Fe accumulation; derepression supports increased Fe accumulation under knockout or Fe deprivation contexts
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PtIRT3 was identified as a direct PtrbHLH011 target by ChIP-seq and promoter repression assays. 2. PtIRT3 expression is reduced in PtrbHLH011 overexpression plants and increased in knockout plants. 3. The paper interprets PtIRT3 as contributing to shoot Fe accumulation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient ChIP-seq; promoter transactivation assay; qRT-PCR in overexpression and knockout plants
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: constitutively expressed in several tissues per cited background; responsive to PtrbHLH011 perturbation in leaf and stem
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate downstream transporter target for improving shoot iron accumulation
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq GEO: GSE265823; transient ChIP-seq GEO: GSE265825
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Figure labels mention ZIP4, but text and accession list support PtIRT3 as the intended target.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Populus tremula × Populus alba; Populus trichocarpa
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: antioxidant and stress-protective metabolites; anthocyanins contribute to leaf color and stress tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Relief of PtrbHLH011-mediated repression is associated with increased flavonoid biosynthesis under Fe deprivation, whereas PtrbHLH011 overexpression represses CHS1 and reduces soluble phenolics and anthocyanin in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CHS1 was identified as a direct repression target of PtrbHLH011 by transient ChIP-seq and promoter transactivation assay. 2. CHS1 expression decreased in PtrbHLH011 overexpression plants and increased in PtrbHLH011 knockout plants. 3. As a chalcone synthase gene, CHS1 links PtrbHLH011 to flavonoid and anthocyanin metabolism under iron deprivation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient ChIP-seq; promoter transactivation assay; qRT-PCR in overexpression and knockout plants; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: leaf and stem expression responsive to PtrbHLH011 perturbation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: first committed step of flavonoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate pathway target for promoter editing or derepression to enhance stress-protective flavonoids under iron-poor conditions
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May shift carbon toward secondary metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq GEO: GSE265823; transient ChIP-seq GEO: GSE265825
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Core pathway role is inferred from gene annotation and paper statement that CHS catalyzes the first step of flavonoid biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Populus tremula × Populus alba; Populus trichocarpa
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: antioxidant and stress-protective metabolites; anthocyanins contribute to leaf color and stress tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Relief of PtrbHLH011-mediated repression is associated with increased flavonoid biosynthesis under Fe deprivation, whereas PtrbHLH011 overexpression represses CHS5 and reduces soluble phenolics and anthocyanin in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CHS5 was identified as a direct repression target of PtrbHLH011 by transient ChIP-seq and promoter transactivation assay. 2. CHS5 expression decreased in PtrbHLH011 overexpression plants and increased in PtrbHLH011 knockout plants. 3. CHS5 contributes to the flavonoid biosynthetic response linked to iron deprivation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient ChIP-seq; promoter transactivation assay; qRT-PCR in overexpression and knockout plants; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: leaf and stem expression responsive to PtrbHLH011 perturbation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: first committed step of flavonoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for promoter editing to boost flavonoid accumulation and oxidative stress tolerance under iron deficiency
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May alter carbon allocation to phenylpropanoid/flavonoid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq GEO: GSE265823; transient ChIP-seq GEO: GSE265825
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper treats CHS1 and CHS5 together as chalcone synthase genes directly repressed by PtrbHLH011.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Populus tremula × Populus alba; Populus trichocarpa
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: lignin/cell wall polymer; mineral nutrient; flavonoid
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: lignin supports secondary cell wall formation and vascular transport; iron is essential micronutrient; flavonoids/anthocyanins protect against oxidative stress
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: secondary cell wall biosynthesis; iron homeostasis; flavonoid biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PtrWND2B; PtrMYB92; CHS1; CHS5; YSL3; PYE; FER1; PtIRT3
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly represses promoters of PtrWND2B, PtrMYB92, CHS1, CHS5, YSL3, PYE, FER1 and PtIRT3
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Central negative regulator linking iron deprivation to activation of SCW biosynthesis, iron accumulation and flavonoid biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Acts as a transcriptional repressor; specific complex partners not established
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: expressed in root, leaf and stem; opposite to SCW marker genes in wood-forming tissues
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: downregulated by iron deprivation in root, leaf, phloem and xylem
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S, constitutive, for overexpression in poplar
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Knockout increases stem diameter, plant height, stem lignin/SCW deposition and leaf Fe accumulation; overexpression decreases growth, lignin/SCW deposition and leaf Fe accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PtrbHLH011 is transcriptionally downregulated by iron deprivation in multiple tissues. 2. PtrbHLH011 knockout enhances growth, lignin deposition, secondary cell wall thickness and leaf iron accumulation, while overexpression causes opposite phenotypes. 3. PtrbHLH011 is a transcriptional repressor that directly binds the AAAGACA motif and represses major regulators/genes for SCW biosynthesis, iron homeostasis and flavonoid biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR cytosine base-editing knockout; constitutive overexpression; histology; lignin quantification; XRF iron imaging; RNA-seq; transient ChIP-seq; promoter transactivation assay; qRT-PCR; EMSA; subcellular localization
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: iron deprivation hydroponic treatment (0 μM Fe versus 10 μM Fe)
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for engineering poplar biomass production and iron accumulation on iron-deficient marginal lands; promoter editing of PtrbHLH011-target relationships is proposed to reduce pleiotropic penalties
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Direct knockout increases lignin content, potentially increasing biomass recalcitrance; pleiotropic effects expected because it co-regulates multiple essential pathways
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq GEO: GSE265823; transient ChIP-seq GEO: GSE265825
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Binds the cis-element AAAGACA by EMSA; close homologue of Arabidopsis AtbHLH011.
